//: Minimal MCP config — fewer servers/tools means fewer tool-definition tokens in EVERY request's system prompt.
//how: Each MCP server injects its tool schemas into the model's context on every turn. Only enable what a session needs, and use a 'tools' allowlist to load a subset.
//tip: Run /context in the CLI to see 'toolDefinitions' token cost. Disable unused servers with /mcp.

microsoft-learn.//tools: Optionally allowlist e.g. ["microsoft_docs_search", "microsoft_docs_fetch"] if you never use code-sample search, to trim tool defs.

mcpServers.//bluebird-note: Heavy code-search servers expose many tools. If you are not doing code-search in a session, comment the server out or remove it to reclaim context tokens.
